import pytest
from source import gcd

def test_gcd_recursive_order_bug():
    # This test exposes the bug where gcd(a % b, b) is called instead of gcd(b, a % b)
    # Using (35, 21) should return 7, but the buggy version will timeout/recursion error
    # due to infinite recursion (a % b = 14, then gcd(14, 21) -> gcd(14, 21) again)
    result = gcd(35, 21)
    assert result == 7, f"gcd(35, 21) should be 7, but got {result}"
